what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a reply to item-bd30758f) in which the author, tim, distinguishes two kinds of typed connections in a graph/link system and sketches how type registries for nodes and edges cascade across nested scopes.
observations: It is a comment reply linked to board://item-bd30758f via commented_on.
It distinguishes directional structural typed edges from domain-specific typed relations.
Typed edges are described as bidirectional, registry/structure-agnostic, and about structures and registries.
Typed relations are described as domain or situation-specific and used for actual graph relations.
It invokes a four-part cascade of registries: global, project, user, instance.
Registries may be embedded into a graph for portability or live in different places and combine/cascade.
The same cascade and registry idea is said to apply to nodes as well as edges.
The author notes there is no pre-existing structured system, only many scattered experiments.
Dragnet is mentioned as a likely tool for eventually capturing those experiments.
The tone is exploratory and terminological rather than prescriptive.
standouts: Explicit vocabulary split between typed edges and typed relations.
Idea that registries can be embedded into graphs for portability.
Dragnet framed as a future capture mechanism for graph experiments.
conventions: Uses noticeboard item YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, and history.
Uses a markdown [point] marker and inline quotation of the prior item.
References another board item by board:// URI.
concerns: Terminology is explicitly not worked out; the edge/relation distinction is asserted but not formally defined.
Cascade scope names are described as not hard rules, making the model fuzzy.
No concrete schema, examples, or code are provided.
notes: Tim's exploratory reply clarifying a proposed split between directional typed edges and domain typed relations, while sketching a four-scope cascading registry model for graph nodes and edges.
questions: What are the exact formal rules that separate typed edges from typed relations?
How do global/project/user/instance registries combine or override each other?
What is a projector channel in this context?
Which existing traversal function is being referenced?
How will dragnet capture and reconcile the scattered graph experiments?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a clarifying, speculative reply about typed edges/relations and cascading registries, not a formal specification.
apparent_themes: Knowledge capture and consolidation of experiments
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to refine the conceptual vocabulary of a link/graph system by separating structural, bidirectional typed edges from domain-specific typed relations, and to describe how type registries can cascade across nested scopes so that diverse graph experiments can eventually be unified or captured by dragnet.
novelty: The deliberate split between structural typed edges and domain typed relations within one system, combined with a four-scope cascade model that allows registries to be embedded into graphs for portability.
contribution: Contributes a vocabulary distinction between edges and relations and a sketch of multi-scope cascading registries that a fused system could keep as design considerations for graph typing, scope inheritance, and portability.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by tim replying to item-bd30758f that distinguishes directional structural typed edges from domain-specific typed relations and outlines a four-scope global/project/user/instance cascading registry model for graph nodes and edges, noting that dragnet may later capture related experiments.
intention_for_embedding: To clarify and explore the conceptual split between structural typed edges and domain typed relations and how their registries cascade across nested scopes.
